title: celtis occidentalis var. crassifolia
intro: Celtis occidentalis var. crassifolia, also known as the Rough-leaf Hackberry, is a variety of the Hackberry tree native to North America, characterized by its distinctive rough leaves and small, yellowish-green fruits. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that can help to soothe and protect the skin. The health benefits of the plant include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties that can aid in the treatment of skin infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically to promote skin health and reduce inflammation.
